name: tdk
tagline: Contribute to culture and industry through creativity
description: TDK Corporation is a leading global electronics company founded in 1935, specializing in passive components, sensors, and power supplies. With a rich history in magnetic materials technology, TDK provides innovative solutions for automotive, industrial, consumer electronics, and information technology markets.
longDescription: TDK Corporation, established in 1935 as the world's first manufacturer of ferrite cores, has evolved into a comprehensive electronic components manufacturer. The company's product portfolio spans passive components including ceramic capacitors, aluminum electrolytic capacitors, film capacitors, and inductors, as well as sensors, power supplies, and magnetic heads. TDK's commitment to innovation is reflected in its extensive R&D investments and global manufacturing footprint across Asia, Europe, and the Americas. The company serves diverse markets including automotive electronics, industrial equipment, renewable energy systems, and consumer electronics. TDK's products are renowned for their high reliability, superior performance, and environmental sustainability, making them the preferred choice for mission-critical applications worldwide.

coreProducts.name: Ceramic Capacitors
coreProducts.description: High-performance MLCCs for automotive, industrial, and consumer applications with industry-leading capacitance density and reliability.

coreProducts.name: Aluminum Electrolytic Capacitors
coreProducts.description: High-capacitance aluminum electrolytic capacitors for power supply filtering, energy storage, and industrial applications.

coreProducts.name: Film Capacitors
coreProducts.description: High-voltage film capacitors for power electronics, renewable energy, and industrial applications with excellent self-healing properties.

coreProducts.name: Inductors and Coils
coreProducts.description: Power inductors, common mode chokes, and high-frequency coils for power management and EMI filtering applications.

industries.name: Automotive Electronics
industries.description: Components for EV/HEV powertrains, ADAS systems, and automotive electronics with AEC-Q200 qualification.

industries.name: Industrial Equipment
industries.description: High-reliability components for industrial automation, motor drives, and power conversion systems.

industries.name: Renewable Energy
industries.description: Capacitors and inductors for solar inverters, wind turbines, and energy storage systems.

industries.name: Consumer Electronics
industries.description: Compact, high-performance components for smartphones, wearables, and home appliances.

certifications.name: IATF 16949
certifications.description: Automotive quality management system certification for automotive component manufacturing.

certifications.name: ISO 9001
certifications.description: International quality management system certification ensuring consistent product quality.

certifications.name: ISO 14001
certifications.description: Environmental management system certification demonstrating commitment to sustainability.

certifications.name: AEC-Q200
certifications.description: Automotive Electronics Council qualification for passive components used in automotive applications.

faqs.question: What are TDK's core competencies in passive components?
faqs.answer: TDK's core competencies stem from its pioneering work in magnetic materials since 1935. The company excels in multilayer ceramic capacitor (MLCC) technology, offering industry-leading capacitance density and reliability. TDK's aluminum electrolytic capacitors feature advanced electrolyte formulations for extended lifetime and high ripple current capability. Their film capacitors utilize proprietary metallization techniques for superior self-healing properties. In inductors, TDK's ferrite core technology enables high-efficiency power conversion. The company's integrated manufacturing capabilities, from raw materials to finished components, ensure consistent quality and supply security.
faqs.decisionGuide: Browse our TDK product categories to find components matching your application requirements, or contact our FAE team for technical recommendations.

faqs.question: How do TDK products compare to other passive component manufacturers?
faqs.answer: TDK distinguishes itself through several key advantages. In ceramic capacitors, TDK offers one of the industry's broadest capacitance and voltage ranges, with automotive-grade MLCCs that meet the most stringent AEC-Q200 requirements. TDK's aluminum electrolytic capacitors provide superior ripple current handling and longer operational life compared to many competitors. The company's film capacitors are preferred in high-voltage applications due to their excellent self-healing characteristics and low loss. TDK's global manufacturing footprint and vertical integration ensure supply stability and competitive pricing. Additionally, TDK's strong R&D investments result in continuous product improvements and innovative solutions.
faqs.decisionGuide: Request samples of TDK components for evaluation in your application, or schedule a technical consultation with our FAE team to compare specifications.

faqs.question: What industries does TDK primarily serve?
faqs.answer: TDK serves a diverse range of industries with specialized component solutions. The automotive sector represents a major market, with TDK providing AEC-Q200 qualified components for EV/HEV powertrains, battery management systems, ADAS, and infotainment systems. Industrial automation and power electronics are key markets, where TDK's high-reliability capacitors and inductors enable efficient motor drives and power conversion. The renewable energy sector relies on TDK's film capacitors for solar inverters and wind turbine power electronics. Consumer electronics, including smartphones, tablets, and wearables, utilize TDK's compact, high-performance MLCCs and inductors. Information technology infrastructure, such as servers and data centers, benefits from TDK's power supply components.
faqs.decisionGuide: Explore our TDK solutions section to find application-specific component recommendations for your industry.

faqs.question: Does TDK offer automotive-qualified components?
faqs.answer: Yes, TDK offers an extensive portfolio of automotive-qualified passive components. Their automotive-grade MLCCs are qualified to AEC-Q200 Rev. E standards and are suitable for critical automotive applications including engine control units, safety systems, and ADAS. TDK's automotive aluminum electrolytic capacitors feature extended temperature ranges and enhanced vibration resistance. The company's automotive inductors meet the stringent reliability requirements for EV/HEV powertrain applications. All automotive-qualified TDK components undergo rigorous testing including temperature cycling, mechanical shock, and high-temperature operational life testing. TDK's IATF 16949 certification ensures consistent quality in automotive component manufacturing.
faqs.decisionGuide: Filter our TDK product listings by automotive qualification, or contact our automotive FAE specialist for component recommendations.

faqs.question: What technical support does TDK provide through distributors?
faqs.answer: Through authorized distributors like BeiLuo, TDK provides comprehensive technical support including component selection guidance, application engineering, and failure analysis. Our FAE team can assist with MLCC derating guidelines, aluminum electrolytic capacitor lifetime calculations, and inductor selection for power applications. TDK provides extensive technical documentation including datasheets, application notes, and spice models. For complex applications, TDK's factory application engineers are available to support through our distribution channel. We also offer design review services to ensure optimal component selection and usage. Technical training on TDK products and technologies can be arranged for customer engineering teams.
faqs.decisionGuide: Submit your technical questions through our support portal, or schedule a consultation with our FAE team for complex application support.
